heading: Foreclosure mitigation efforts
label: 5219 Foreclosure mitigation efforts
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a)(1). inserted before period at end “and to remediate lead and asbestos hazards in residential properties”.Pub. L. 115–1742009—Subsec. (a). designated existing provisions as par. (1), inserted par. (1) heading, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 111–5
amendments: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a)(1). inserted before period at end “and to remediate lead and asbestos hazards in residential properties”.Pub. L. 115–1742009—Subsec. (a). designated existing provisions as par. (1), inserted par. (1) heading, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 111–5

children.heading: Residential mortgage loan servicing standards
children.label: a Residential mortgage loan servicing standards

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To the extent that the Secretary acquires mortgages, mortgage backed securities, and other assets secured by residential real estate, including multifamily housing, the Secretary shall implement a plan that seeks to maximize assistance for homeowners and use the authority of the Secretary to encourage the servicers of the underlying mortgages, considering net present value to the taxpayer, to take advantage of the HOPE for Homeowners Program under or other available programs to minimize foreclosures. In addition, the Secretary may use loan guarantees and credit enhancements to facilitate loan modifications to prevent avoidable foreclosures and to remediate lead and asbestos hazards in residential properties.1section 1715z–23 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “mortgage-backed”.1

children.heading: Waiver of certain provisions in connection with loan modifications
children.label: 2 Waiver of certain provisions in connection with loan modifications
children.text: The Secretary shall not be required to apply executive compensation restrictions under , or to receive warrants or debt instruments under , solely in connection with any loan modification under this section.section 5221 of this titlesection 5223 of this title

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: b Coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate with the Corporation, the Board (with respect to any mortgage or mortgage-backed securities or pool of securities held, owned, or controlled by or on behalf of a Federal reserve bank, as provided in ), the Federal Housing Finance Agency, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, and other Federal Government entities that hold troubled assets to attempt to identify opportunities for the acquisition of classes of troubled assets that will improve the ability of the Secretary to improve the loan modification and restructuring process and, where permissible, to permit bona fide tenants who are current on their rent to remain in their homes under the terms of the lease. In the case of a mortgage on a residential rental property, the plan required under this section shall include protecting Federal, State, and local rental subsidies and protections, and ensuring any modification takes into account the need for operating funds to maintain decent and safe conditions at the property.section 5220(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Consent to reasonable loan modification requests
children.label: c Consent to reasonable loan modification requests
children.text: Upon any request arising under existing investment contracts, the Secretary shall consent, where appropriate, and considering net present value to the taxpayer, to reasonable requests for loss mitigation measures, including term extensions, rate reductions, principal write downs, increases in the proportion of loans within a trust or other structure allowed to be modified, or removal of other limitation on modifications.
